chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 6548 of 2019 ___________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge(s) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders as to maintainability of petition. 03.02.2025 Mr. Arsalan Wahid, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Aamir Raza, advocate for the respondent 3. Mr. Ali Almani, advocate and Mr. Furqan Mushtaq, advocate for the respondent 4. Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Assistant Attorney General. Per learned counsel, petitioner is aggrieved by certain charges being recovered by the respondent 4, admittedly a private party not amenable to writ jurisdiction. Sindh Sales Tax invoice, available at page 43 of the court file, has been pointed out to demonstrate charges, however, the same demonstrates that same was issued to the petitioner and has been duly paid. There is no suggestion of the petitioner having been aggrieved of the same at any stage before payment and the payment is also not said to be under any protest. The other invoice is referred to, at page 83 of the court file, issued by the Karachi Port Trust, however, the said entity is not even arrayed as respondent herein. In view hereof, it is prima facie apparent that writ is sought against a private party, no cause of action has been demonstrated, therefore, the petition is found to be misconceived, frivolous, hence, dismissed. Judge Judge Khuhro/PA
